questions.question: A figure has _______ symmetry if it can be folded so that the two halves match exactly.
questions.explanation: Line symmetry (or reflection symmetry) is when one half of a figure is a mirror image of the other.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular square have?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: two passing through the midpoints of opposite sides and two through the diagonals.

questions.question: Which of the following English letters has only horizontal line symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter 'E' can be divided into matching top and bottom halves by a horizontal line.

questions.question: The order of rotational symmetry for a circle is:
questions.explanation: A circle looks the same after rotation by any angle around its center.

questions.question: An equilateral triangle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, each altitude acts as a line of symmetry.

questions.question: Which of these shapes has rotational symmetry of order 2?
questions.explanation: A rectangle looks the same at 180° and 360° rotations.

questions.question: A Scalene triangle has ______ lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: A scalene triangle has no equal sides or angles, hence no line of symmetry.

questions.question: The angle of rotation for a figure with rotational symmetry of order 4 is:
questions.explanation: Angle of rotation = 360° / Order = 360 / 4 = 90°.

questions.question: Which of the following digits has rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: The digit '8' (when written symmetrically) looks the same after a 180° rotation.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular hexagon have?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon with 'n' sides has 'n' lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Which letter has both horizontal and vertical lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter 'X' can be folded both vertically and horizontally to match perfectly.

questions.question: If a shape looks exactly the same after a 180° turn, its order of rotational symmetry is at least:
questions.explanation: Looking same at 180° and 360° makes the order 2.

questions.question: A semi-circle has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A semi-circle has only one line of symmetry passing through the center of the diameter.

questions.question: Which of the following shapes has no line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A general parallelogram has rotational symmetry but no lines of symmetry.

questions.question: What is the order of rotational symmetry for a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: A regular polygon of 'n' sides has rotational symmetry of order 'n'.

questions.question: The line of symmetry is also known as a ______ line.
questions.explanation: Because it acts like a mirror reflecting one half onto the other.

questions.question: Which letter has rotational symmetry but no line symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter 'S' can be rotated 180° to look the same, but cannot be folded to match.

questions.question: A line of symmetry of a circle always passes through its:
questions.explanation: Any diameter of a circle is its line of symmetry, and all diameters pass through the center.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular octagon have?
questions.explanation: Octagon has 8 sides, so a regular octagon has 8 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Every object has at least rotational symmetry of order:
questions.explanation: Order 1 means it looks the same only after a full 360° turn.

questions.question: An isosceles triangle has ______ line(s) of symmetry.
questions.explanation: The line of symmetry connects the vertex between equal sides to the midpoint of the base.

questions.question: The angle of rotation for a regular hexagon is:
Which of these figures has exactly 2 lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has two lines of symmetry connecting the midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.question: A rhombus has lines of symmetry along its:
questions.explanation: The two diagonals of a rhombus are its lines of symmetry.

questions.question: If we rotate a shape by 120° and it looks the same, its order of rotational symmetry is:
